tasks.label: Arrange contents insurance from day one of the tenancy
Terminate or move (if possible) your existing broadband account
Book broadband install for move-in day or shortly thereafter
tasks.dueText: 1+ month ahead, but may be blocked by an existing active account

tasks.label: Arrange moving-van access and any parking permit you need (e.g. with the local authority if on-street, or with building management if available)
Set up mail redirection with your postal service
Collect all keys and fobs: front door, back door, windows, mailbox, communal, garage/parking, bin store, cycle store, alarm codes
Test every key/fob in every lock
Test the door entry / intercom / buzzer system and any communal-door fobs
Take date-stamped photos of gas, electricity, water and heating meters
tasks.dueText: Ensure the meter serial number is visible

tasks.label: Check for any outstanding debt left by prior tenants if any meter is prepayment/key/card/pay-as-you-go
Take a video walkthrough of every room, narrating and zooming in on any defects
Photograph inside the oven, fridge, freezer and its drawers, washing machine drum and seal, and dishwasher filter
Photograph inside every cupboard, wardrobe and drawer across all rooms
Photograph any damage to floors, walls, skirting, ceilings, doors, windows and garden (scuffs, marks, scratches, stains, mould, chipped tiles, burns or worn or bald patches)
Photograph all curtains, blinds and lampshades (fixed and portable)
Check the inventory line by line against actual item condition -- note where condition is worse than stated, or where photos are not high enough resolution to show damage
Test all smoke alarms and carbon monoxide (CO) alarms
Locate the water stopcock / shut-off valve, gas shut-off valve and the fuse board / consumer unit
tasks.dueText: Ask the landlord or letting agent if not obvious

tasks.label: Test the boiler, hot water, heating and every individual radiator
Run every tap (including outdoor taps and showers/baths) and flush every toilet
Test every hob ring, oven, extractor fan, microwave, kettle and other provided kitchen appliances
Test all bulbs, including within the extractor fan and any workspace / countertop lighting
Check every window opens, closes and locks
Check for damp, mould and pest evidence (droppings or smell behind furniture / major appliances)
Check in your contract or confirm the landlord/letting agent emergency and out-of-hours repair contact details
Submit your own inventory amendment promptly with all comments and photos by email
Open or move accounts and submit meter readings to gas, electricity, water and sewerage suppliers (and any communal/district heating provider, if applicable)
Find out refuse and recycling collection days and bin/recycling store arrangements
Register for any local property/residence tax or municipal charges, and apply for any discounts or exemptions you qualify for (e.g. single-occupant, student, low-income, disability)
Transfer or register any TV/broadcasting licence or media fee that applies in your country
Register with a local doctor/GP and dentist
Update your nominated pharmacy with your new address for repeat prescription delivery
Set up a standing order or recurring payment for rent
Update all banks, building societies, mortgage lenders and credit cards with your new address
Update savings, investment, exchange and brokerage accounts with your new address
Update life insurance, critical illness and income protection with your new address
Update solicitor, accountant and will/executor records with your new address
Update your driving licence address with the relevant authority
tasks.dueText: Some jurisdictions fine you for not updating it promptly

tasks.label: Update your vehicle registration / log book with the relevant authority
Update your vehicle insurance address
Update any disability parking permit or badge with the relevant authority
Update workplace and personal pension providers with your new address
Notify your tax authority of your address change (income tax, social security/national insurance and any tax credits)
Update any government benefits, pension, student finance or social security bodies you deal with
Update your business/company registration address (registered office and director's service address) if you run a business
Update your employer's records with your new address
Update vet records and pet microchip database if you have pets
Register on the electoral register, where applicable
Update default shipping addresses with frequently used online retailers and payment services
Update gym and other subscription services (e.g. mobile provider) with your new address
Check your local deposit protection rules and any legal cap on the deposit amount, and confirm you were not charged any prohibited letting fees
Confirm how your deposit is held/protected and obtain written confirmation and an inventory/condition report

Obtain any legally required safety certificates and compliance documents for your jurisdiction (e.g. gas, electrical, energy efficiency) and confirm the landlord is licensed/registered if required locally
